content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

October 4, 1988

Wilson Mallery
Honey Lake Resource Conservation District
50 B Hall Street
Susanville, CA 96130

Re: Your Request for Assistance
Our Advice File No. I-88-335

Dear Mr. Mallery:

On August 3, 1988, you wrote to us requesting an exemption
for the Honey Lake Resource Conservation District from the
requirement that they adopt a conflict of interest code under
the Political Reform Act.

Your letter does not indicate whether the Honey Lake
Resource Conservation District is a single-county or a
multi-county district. A single-county district is located
entirely within one county, while a multi-county district is
located in two or more counties. If the district is a
single-county district, the request for exemption should be
directed to that county's board of supervisors, which would be
your code reviewing body. (Section 82011(b).) The county may
have an established exemption policy for you to follow or may
choose to use the same criteria set out in Regulation 18751.

If the Honey Lake Resource Conservation District is a
multi-county district, its request for exemption is
appropriately directed to the Fair Political Practices
Commission, the code reviewing body for multi-county agencies
(Section 82011(a).) Multi-county districts must meet the
criteria and follow the procedure set forth in Regulation
18751. That regulation is attached.

Based upon the information you provided in your letter, it
appears your district is the type of district intended to be
covered by the exemption policy. The question at this point is

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Wilson Mallery
October 4, 1988
Page -2-


where to direct your request for exemption. If I can help any
further in this regard, please feel free to call me at (916)
322-5901.

Sincerely,

Diane M. Griffiths
General Counsel

[signature]
By: Jeanette E. Turvill
Political Reform Consultant
Legal Division

JET/jt

88-535

HONEY LAKE VALLEY RESOURCE CONSERVATION DISTRICT    50 B HALL STREET    SUSANVILLE, CA. 96130


August 3, 1988

State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
PO Box 807
Sacramento, Ca. 95804

Dear Mr. Brown,

The Honey Lake Valley Resource Conservation District has
been trying to obtain an exemption from the requirement that
it adopt a Conflict of Interest Code. We have contacted the
County Clerk's office and she suggested that we take the
route of the Pit Resource Conservation District and contact
you to try to get the same exemption.

Our budget is $8400 per year which is funded through the
County. Our expenditures are usually $5500 with the
remaining monies being in contingency.

We employ one part-time secretary, who is paid jointly
through the Soil Conservation Service and RCD. We have five
Board members who are local farmers and ranchers. They
receive no wages.

We would be more than happy to send you any further
information that you may need in this matter.

Enclosed please find a copy of the letter that was sent to
the Pit RCD in 1978 granting them an extension. Thank you
for your time.


Sincerely,

Wilson Mallery
President

State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 207 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1400 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance Administration          Execution Legal          Enforcement          Conflict of Interest
(916) 322 5660                                322 5931                 322 6441            322 4444

August 11, 1978


Pit Resource Conservation District

     At its meeting of ___August 2, 1978____, the Fair
Political Practices Commission voted to exempt your agency
from the requirement that it adopt a Conflict of Interest
Code pursuant to Government Code §87300. This action was
taken based on the size and functions of your agency. It
was found that it was unnecessary to adopt a Code, because
if one were approved there would legally be no "designated
employees". See Government Code §82019 and 2 Cal. Adm.
Code §18751.

     The Commission treated your submission of the Code
constructively as a request for an exemption pursuant
to 2 Cal. Adm. Cod §18751. No further action is necessary
by you at this time. However, if the agency greatly in-
creases the size of its budget, or changes the nature of
its governmental functions, you should so inform us.

     We apologize for the delay in resolving this matter,
but trust it is resolved to your satisfaction. Thank you
for your cooperation, and if we can be of any further
assistance in this or any other matter, please feel free
to contact our office.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]
                                        Ted Prim, Chief
                                        Conflicts of Interest Division

TP:KG:nw                               ~

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

August 31, 1988


Wilson Mallery, President
Honey Lake Valley Resource
Conservation District
50 B Hall Street
Susanville, CA  96130

Re:  88-335

Dear Mr. Mallery:

Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform
Act was received on August 26, 1988 by the Fair Political
Practices Commission.  If you have any questions about your
advice request, you may contact Jeanette Turvill, in the Legal
Division, directly at (916) 322-5901.

We try to answer all advice requests promptly.  Therefore,
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions,
or more information is needed, you should expect a response
within 21 working days if your request seeks formal written
advice.  If more information is needed, we will contact you
shortly to advise you as to the information needed.  If your
request is for informal assistance, we will answer it as
quickly as we can.  (See Commission Regulation 18329 (2 Cal.
Code of Regs. Sec. 18329).)

You also should be aware that your letter and our response
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon
receipt of a proper request for disclosure.

Very truly yours,


Diane M. Griffiths
General Counsel

sections.question_synthetic: Does the Honey Lake Resource Conservation District need to submit its request for exemption from the conflict of interest code requirement to the county board of supervisors or to the FPPC, and what criteria must it meet?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The appropriate reviewing body depends on whether the district is single-county or multi-county. If single-county, the request should go to the county board of supervisors; if multi-county, it should go to the FPPC and must meet the criteria in Regulation 18751. Based on the information provided, the district appears to be the type intended to be covered by an exemption policy.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains multiple pieces: main advice letter from October 4, 1988, an unrelated historical exemption letter from August 11, 1978, and an August 31, 1988 acknowledgment letter. Extraction focuses on the primary October 4, 1988 advice letter. No verbatim QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections present; synthesized versions provided based on letter content.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Does the Honey Lake Resource Conservation District need to submit its request for exemption from the conflict of interest code requirement to the county board of supervisors or to the FPPC, and what criteria must it meet?

CONCLUSION: The appropriate reviewing body depends on whether the district is single-county or multi-county. If single-county, the request should go to the county board of supervisors; if multi-county, it should go to the FPPC and must meet the criteria in Regulation 18751. Based on the information provided, the district appears to be the type intended to be covered by an exemption policy.

embedding.summary: The FPPC responds to a request for exemption from conflict of interest code requirements, clarifying the jurisdictional distinction between single-county and multi-county resource conservation districts and directing the requestor to the appropriate reviewing body.
